description: Step-by-step craft procedures for common electrical tasks

steps: Review pull ticket: verify cable size, type, from/to, reel number, route, estimated length +10%
Walk the route: inspect conduit, caps removed, pull rope in place, no obstructions
Set up reel on jacks: cable pays off top (clockwise), reel braked
Position sheaves/rollers at all bends — min diameter per manufacturer
Attach pulling grip with swivel to prevent twist
Apply lubricant generously at feed point and each bend
Establish radio communication between all pull points
Pull at slow steady pace — monitor tension, don't exceed sidewall bearing pressure
Feeders guide cable, prevent kinks, maintain back-tension
Leave service loops (10-15 ft each end for termination)
Cut cable, apply tags at both ends immediately
Seal conduit ends with duct seal
Complete pull ticket with actual length, date, crew

conduit_bending.offset_procedure: Measure offset depth → multiply by multiplier for distance between bends → calculate shrinkage → mark and bend

conduit_support_spacing.RMC: 10 ft and within 3 ft of boxes
conduit_support_spacing.EMT: 10 ft and within 3 ft of boxes

steps: Verify motor nameplate matches spec: HP, voltage, phase, RPM, FLA
Verify rotation requirement from P&ID or data sheet
Megger test motor windings before connecting — record values
Verify wire size matches motor FLA per NEC 430
Route conductors into junction box — maintain bend radius
Connect per wiring diagram on nameplate (high vs low voltage config)
Anti-oxidant on aluminum conductors
Torque all connections per manufacturer spec — document
Verify equipment grounding conductor connected
Install strain relief at junction box entry
Bump test: momentary start to check rotation
If wrong rotation: swap any two phases at STARTER (not motor)
Check for vibration, noise, or heat during bump test
Document rotation, megger values, torque values, wire tags
De-energize and LOTO circuit under test
Disconnect cable from all equipment at both ends
Verify all conductors isolated
Set up barriers and warning signs
Select proper test voltage
Test each conductor to ground individually
Test each conductor to every other conductor (phase-to-phase)
Apply voltage for 1 minute (or per spec)
Record reading — should be stable or increasing
Discharge cable: ground for 4x test duration
Verify zero volts before removing leads
Record all values on test form

acceptable_values.new_cable: Hundreds of megohms to gigohms expected

quality_common_punch_items: Missing ground connections or bonding jumpers
Cable tray splices missing bonding jumpers
Missing pull wire in spare conduits
